summary: Cleaned‑up soundboard capture of a classic early‑1970 show featuring a killer acoustic set and a smoking Lovelight/Viola Lee Blues performance.
review: The new SBD transfer dramatically reduces tape hiss and brings out instrument separation, though occasional distortion and a low Pigpen organ mix keep it from flawless. Musically the concert is an above‑average early‑’70 outing – the acoustic set shines with Deep Elem Blues and Friend of the Devil, while the electric half delivers a ferocious Viola Lee Blues and a standout Lovelight with classic Pig Pen rap.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance and highlight several songs, but note recording flaws (hiss, distortion, low organ) that prevent a top rating; weighted average of show (70%) and sound quality (30%) yields a solid above‑average score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reduced tape hiss and clearer instrument separation on the new transfer; occasional distortion, a cut in Black Peter, vocal mix louder than instruments, and Pig Pen's organ low in the mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong acoustic first set (Deep Elem, Friend of the Devil, etc.) followed by a solid electric portion; highlights include a blazing Viola Lee Blues and a jam‑filled Lovelight, though a tape cut affects Black Peter.

band_member_comments.Phil: Sings loudly and on key; his high harmonies are noted but often get buried in the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pig Pen's organ sits low in the mix, though his vocal rap on Lovelight is a highlight.
